summary: Iteration 49 - Aditivo Arquitetural 002: Backend role-based visibility / RBAC scoping. 17/17 pytest cases PASS on https://procure-manutrix.preview.emergentagent.com. Verified that GET /api/ordens-servico, GET /api/dashboard/stats, GET /api/dashboard/os-por-disciplina and GET /api/kpis correctly scope results per role for master/admin/pcm/supervisor(mecânico)/supervisor(elétrico+instrumentação)/técnico(mecânico)/técnico(elétrico)/operador. Critical security rule confirmed: Operador NEVER receives OS with disciplina in {mecanica, eletrica, instrumentacao} — only producao+civil (2 OS). POST /api/ordens-servico denormalises sector_id from the asset onto the OS doc. Login page loads.
action_items: (Optional) /api/dashboard/stats payload key for open OS is `ordens_servico.abertas` (nested). Confirm frontend reads this nested path — flat key `os_abertas` does NOT exist. Same for `/api/kpis` where backlog is `backlog_total`. Document the contract for the frontend team.
critical_code_review_comments: deps.py:260-263 — técnico fallback (when no disciplinas AND no areas) uses {'equipe': user_id} for OS but $or list construction has a ternary that always evaluates inline, OK functionally but dense; consider explicit branching for readability.

deps.py:268-285 — operador visibility uses 'disciplina': {'$in': [..., None, '']} to include legacy/undisciplined records. This is correct for backward compat, but going forward all OS MUST have disciplina set (current 5 seeded OS all have it). Recommend a migration that backfills disciplina=producao on legacy OS so the None/'' clause can be removed.

server.py:2026 seed_test_users uses area_ids slicing (areas[:2], areas[1:3], areas[:1]) — depends on the sectors list being ordered the same way (PLANTA-01,02,03). Currently relies on MongoDB natural ordering. For determinism, sort by `nome` before slicing.

deps.py:206 supervisor / 234 tecnico scope_filter combines disciplina AND ativo_id via the SAME dict ('AND' semantics) — correct. But the OR with direct assignment ({responsavel_id}, {equipe}) means a user assigned to an out-of-scope OS would still see it. That is by design (assignee override). Worth documenting in OpenAPI / user docs.

build_dashboard_visibility (deps.py:297) does NOT include the direct-assignment OR like the list endpoint does, so a técnico assigned to an OS outside their disciplina/area will see it in /ordens-servico but the dashboard counts will exclude it — inconsistency. Consider unifying or documenting.
POST /api/ordens-servico correctly denormalises sector_id. Verified via test_create_os_includes_sector_id_from_ativo. Make sure all future writes (PUT/PATCH) also re-apply this when ativo_id changes.
test_credentials: All credentials from review_request work. Master seeded the 7 test users via POST /api/seed/test-users (status: updated for all 7).
seed_data_creation: POST /api/seed/test-users re-applied disciplines/areas to test.* users. test_create_os_includes_sector_id_from_ativo creates a TEST_VIS_SECTOR_DENORM OS and cleans it up via DELETE. No residual TEST data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Visibility engine (deps.build_visibility_query / build_dashboard_visibility) is fully operational across master/admin/pcm/supervisor/tecnico/inspetor/operador. The 5 seeded OS span all 5 disciplines (mecanica/eletrica/instrumentacao/producao/civil) across 3 areas (PLANTA-01=0737a6a1, PLANTA-02=84436169, PLANTA-03=5c79c7fb). Dashboard stats response is NESTED: data.ordens_servico.abertas (not flat os_abertas). KPIs response uses backlog_total. /api/dashboard/os-por-disciplina returns a list of {disciplina, count}. Inspecoes/exports visibility was not retested in this iteration; previous iteration_48 covered different scope.
rca of the issue: n/a — feature works as specified. The 4 initial test failures were due to wrong response-key assumptions in the test (flat keys vs nested `ordens_servico.abertas` / `backlog_total`). Test asserts were corrected; underlying backend behaviour was always correct.
